fabric_texture_bank_v1.description: Fabric + texture intelligence to make styling feedback sound premium. Focuses on drape, structure, finish (matte/shine), and how fabrics read on camera/in real life.

positive_premium_signals: That fabric reads expensive—clean drape, no fuss.
The texture adds quiet richness without trying too hard.
This finish looks premium and intentional.
The fabric movement gives it that elevated feel.
This texture contrast is doing a lot of work—in a good way.
The material looks structured enough to hold shape nicely.
This fabric choice makes the whole outfit feel more refined.
The finish is clean—nothing looks flimsy or noisy.
That fabric has a nice weight to it, which looks polished.
positive_drape_structure: The drape is smooth, so the silhouette reads clean.
This holds structure well—it keeps the look sharp.
The fabric falls nicely, which makes it feel effortless.
That material has good shape memory—stays neat.
The fabric weight gives the outfit a confident outline.
This skims instead of clinging, which looks very polished.
The structure makes the look feel intentional.
That drape creates a soft, elegant line.
This fabric makes layering look cleaner.
positive_shine_matte_balance: The shine level is controlled—elevated, not loud.
That matte finish makes it feel modern and expensive.
The subtle sheen adds a dressy edge without overdoing it.
The mix of matte + a hint of shine is very premium.
That satin/shine moment works because the rest is simple.
The finish looks smooth and high-quality.
This texture catches light in a flattering way.
The shine is giving ‘special occasion’ in a soft way.
This matte texture makes the look feel grounded and chic.
constructive_fabric_noise_or_cheap_read: The base works, but the fabric is reading a little ‘noisy’ on camera.
This looks good—just slightly flimsy in the finish.
The texture is adding a bit more chaos than the outfit needs.
The fabric is making the look feel less polished than it could.
This finish is slightly crumple-prone, so it can look untidy fast.
The shine level is a touch high, so it can read louder than intended.
The fabric is clinging in a way that breaks the clean line.
The material looks a bit thin for the vibe you’re going for.
The texture is competing with the silhouette a little.
This would look more premium with a cleaner fabric finish.
refinement_suggestions: If you swap one piece to a heavier/cleaner fabric, the whole look levels up.
Keeping one texture as the hero and the rest matte will make it feel expensive.
A quick steam will instantly make this read more premium.
If the fabric is shiny, pair it with a matte shoe/bag so it feels balanced.
If the fabric is clingy, adding a structured layer can clean up the line.
Try one textured piece max—everything else smooth and simple.
If you want it sharper, choose a fabric that holds shape (denim, suiting, crisp cotton).
If you want it softer, choose a fabric with drape (viscose, crepe, soft knit).
A clean lining/inner layer can make this sit better and look pricier.
If you reduce wrinkles and tidy hems, this looks instantly elevated.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 fabric insight. Add 1 refinement only if user asks to elevate or says it feels off.
avoid: Overly technical textile talk unless user asks.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Reaction → Fabric/texture observation → Optional refinement → Invitation closer.
